project.name: course-dev-ide-with-agents
project.description: Collaborative intelligence platform for course content production — a multi-agent orchestrator (Marcus) + per-specialist 9-node LangGraph scaffold + tamper-evident operator-verdict ledger, built on LangChain/LangGraph. Scope: app/ + scripts/ + skills/ (code-only narrow scan).

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the manifest subsystem, re-exporting load, compile, and exception types as the public manifest API.

nodes.name: compiler.py
nodes.summary: Core manifest compiler that validates pipeline manifests and assembles a LangGraph StateGraph from node/edge specs, resolving specialist dispatch handlers and gate codes.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of private helper functions for stepwise validation (gate codes, model refs, cycles, conditions); central compile()/compile_run_graph() entry points orchestrate the whole pipeline.

nodes.name: conditions.py
nodes.summary: Condition function registry resolving named conditional-edge predicates (e.g., always_true, always_false) for the manifest compiler.

nodes.name: exceptions.py
nodes.summary: Domain exception classes for manifest validation, compilation, and schema-import failures used across the manifest subsystem.

nodes.name: gate_fold_manifest_emit.py
nodes.summary: CLI tool and library that emits a derived gate-fold manifest YAML from the pipeline manifest, projecting fold/section relationships with deterministic generation timestamps.

nodes.name: gate_topology.py
nodes.summary: CLI renderer that prints pipeline gate topology (flat or audit-cross-referenced) from manifest YAML files for operator visibility.

nodes.name: lanes.py
nodes.summary: Lane-aware compiler facade that compiles manifests via either the substrate or production compilation paths based on validation mode.

nodes.name: loader.py
nodes.summary: YAML manifest loader that reads pipeline manifest files and validates them via Pydantic, raising ManifestValidationError on failure.

nodes.name: refs.py
nodes.summary: Dotted-reference resolver that dynamically imports a module attribute and verifies it matches expected base class(es), used for resolving decision card schemas.

nodes.name: schema.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 schema definitions for pipeline manifests, including NodeSpec, EdgeSpec, PipelineManifest, and learning-events configs with cross-field validators.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy Pydantic v2 model_validator usage for cross-field invariants (unique node IDs, entrypoint resolution, edge endpoint resolution, fold-field mutual exclusion).

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Marcus orchestrator package init providing the NegotiatorSeam type for tracking pending operator dialogue state during orchestration.

nodes.name: routing.py
nodes.summary: Manifest-driven routing logic that decides the next graph node from the current node, producing a RoutingDecision with target specialist and dispatch envelope.

nodes.name: supervisor.py
nodes.summary: Supervisor abstraction that maps Marcus preset to a runtime mode and drives a single routing step against the manifest.

nodes.name: ping.py
nodes.summary: MCP tool registration that exposes a 'ping' tool returning a minimal-node echo response for runtime health verification.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic models for the pipeline model registry, defining RegistryEntry (per-model metadata) and PipelineRegistry (catalog with default-model invariant).

nodes.name: registry_check.py
nodes.summary: CLI validator that loads the model registry YAML, validates it through PipelineRegistry, and reports counts/errors to operators.

nodes.name: selection_policy.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic models for the model-selection policy: SelectionRule entries with tier preferences plus a ModelSelectionPolicy enforcing rule-id uniqueness and no silent overlap.

nodes.name: selector.py
nodes.summary: Model resolution orchestrator that resolves a specialist+node request to a concrete model using per-call overrides, specialist config, auto-select policy, and registry availability with cache-prefix hashing.
nodes.languageNotes: Layered fallback chain: per-call override → per-node override → specialist default → auto-select → ModelResolutionError; each layer emits provenance via ModelResolutionEntry.

nodes.name: specialist_model_config.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for per-specialist model configuration including default model, per-node overrides, and default temperature.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Replay package barrel using lazy __getattr__ to defer importing discovery/regression submodules until first access.

nodes.name: discovery.py
nodes.summary: Trial discovery layer that scans the baseline directory for closed-trial envelopes and yields TrialRef objects with envelope/metadata paths and sanctum fingerprints.

nodes.name: regression.py
nodes.summary: Replay regression harness that re-runs closed trials and detects manifest/pack/sanctum drift, exposing CLI + library APIs with budget enforcement and provenance logging.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines a typed error hierarchy (ReplayError → PackHashDrift/SanctumFingerprintDrift/ManifestSnapshotDrift/ReplayBudgetExceeded) plus result dataclasses for batch + per-trial outcomes.

nodes.name: compiled_graph_digest.py
nodes.summary: Canonical-JSON digest computer that hashes the compiled graph (manifest + pack version + dispatch registry) into a stable sha256 fingerprint for drift detection.

nodes.name: minimal_node.py
nodes.summary: Trivial pass-through graph node that echoes input back to output, used as a minimal substrate for smoke tests and MCP ping tooling.

nodes.name: server.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI runtime app exposing /health (with Postgres connectivity probe) and /invoke (executes the minimal node) endpoints.

nodes.name: runtime_server.py
nodes.summary: Uvicorn entry-point script that resolves the runtime port from RUNTIME_PORT env var and launches the FastAPI runtime server.

nodes.name: smoke_test.py
nodes.summary: End-to-end smoke-test CLI that loads a manifest, compiles a substrate (or full) LangGraph from it, and invokes it with sample input to verify the pipeline boots.

nodes.name: compile
nodes.summary: Top-level manifest compiler: runs all validators (frozen graph version, model refs, gate codes, conditions, decision-card schemas, dependency cycles) and assembles a LangGraph StateGraph.

nodes.name: compile_run_graph
nodes.summary: Production-mode graph compiler that also loads the specialist dispatch registry to wire real handlers into the compiled StateGraph.

nodes.name: _add_node_and_edges

nodes.name: specialist_envelope.py

nodes.name: specialist_return.py

nodes.languageNotes: Uses a model-level validator (_check_invariants) to enforce verb-payload consistency via the dedicated validator module.

nodes.name: SpecialistReturn
nodes.summary: Base return model for specialist verbs (accept/edit/reject) carrying the verb, payload, optional edit_payload, optional reject_reason, and timezone-aware timestamp.

nodes.name: specialist_return_validators.py
nodes.summary: Cross-field validator enforcing verb-payload invariants for SpecialistReturn — edit verbs require edit_payload, reject verbs require reject_reason, and non-applicable fields must be absent.

nodes.name: enforce_specialist_verb_payload_consistency
nodes.summary: Raises ValueError if a SpecialistReturn's verb does not match the populated optional fields (edit_payload for edit, reject_reason for reject).

nodes.name: _act.py
nodes.summary: Wanda specialist act-orchestrator: decodes the envelope payload, generates Wondercraft audio beds for storyboard segments, and emits a compositor invocation plus model-resolution trail entries on the run state.
nodes.languageNotes: Module-level helpers cooperate with the WondercraftClient + dispatch shim to produce SFX/music beds keyed by storyboard slides.

nodes.name: WandaActError
nodes.summary: Domain-specific RuntimeError raised when Wanda's act phase encounters an invalid envelope payload or audio-bed generation failure.

nodes.name: decode_envelope_payload
nodes.summary: Parses the JSON-encoded payload off the run-state envelope and validates that it is a dict before returning it for downstream Wanda processing.

nodes.name: _narration_text
nodes.summary: Extracts a usable narration string from heterogeneous payload shapes (raw string, manifest dict rows, narration metadata) for audio-bed prompt construction.

nodes.name: _bed_specs
nodes.summary: Builds a list of audio-bed specifications from the storyboard's audio track, applying safe defaults for id, capability, and duration on each spec.

nodes.name: _call_generate_audio_bed
nodes.summary: Invokes the Wondercraft client to create a scripted-podcast audio bed for a single spec, then waits for the async job to finish before returning the merged result dict.

nodes.name: _audio_bytes
nodes.summary: Resolves audio payload bytes from Wondercraft results, accepting raw bytes, str payloads, or falling back to a marker-based placeholder when only URLs/job IDs are returned.

nodes.name: generate_audio_beds
nodes.summary: Iterates each bed spec, dispatches to Wondercraft, writes audio bytes to disk under the beds directory, and assembles the audio-tracks list for the compositor invocation.

nodes.name: act
nodes.summary: Wanda's LangGraph node entry point: orchestrates payload decoding, audio-bed generation, model-resolution-trail bookkeeping, and CacheState emission for the run state.

nodes.name: wondercraft_dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Capability dispatcher that maps Wanda's audio-capability codes (EP, DP, AS, MB, CM, AH) to specific Wondercraft API calls (podcast episode, dialogue, summary, music bed, chapter markers, assembly handoff).
nodes.languageNotes: Function-table style: per-capability private handlers + central dispatch_to_wondercraft router.

nodes.name: WondercraftDispatchError
nodes.summary: RuntimeError subtype raised when a dispatch capability code is unknown or its payload fails validation.

nodes.name: _ep_podcast_episode_produce
nodes.summary: Capability handler EP: validates the prompt + voice id payload and invokes WondercraftClient.create_podcast to produce a podcast episode.

nodes.name: _dp_podcast_dialogue_produce
nodes.summary: Capability handler DP: validates script + voice mapping and invokes WondercraftClient.create_conversation_podcast for multi-speaker dialogue output.

nodes.name: _as_audio_summary_produce
nodes.summary: Capability handler AS: validates summary script + voice id and invokes WondercraftClient.create_scripted_podcast for a single-voice audio summary.

nodes.name: _mb_music_bed_apply
nodes.summary: Capability handler MB: performs a connectivity probe to Wondercraft and returns a music-bed application stub payload (no upstream music API yet).

nodes.name: _cm_chapter_markers_emit
nodes.summary: Capability handler CM: derives chapter markers (id, timestamp, title) from the segments list of the supplied payload and returns them as a structured response.

nodes.name: _ah_audio_assembly_handoff
nodes.summary: Capability handler AH: emits a compositor-handoff envelope referencing the produced bed assets and a UTC ISO timestamp for downstream assembly.

nodes.name: dispatch_to_wondercraft
nodes.summary: Central dispatcher: normalizes the capability code, instantiates a WondercraftClient when needed, and routes to the matching private handler with structured error wrapping.

nodes.name: AuthenticationError

nodes.name: RateLimitError

nodes.languageNotes: Uses importlib to dynamically load the preclosure hook from skills/bmad-agent-cora rather than a static import, decoupling app code from skill packaging.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: Cora dev-graph manifest loader and compiler: validates the YAML dev-graph schema with strict Pydantic models, resolves dotted handler refs, and assembles a LangGraph StateGraph keyed by StoryState.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of Pydantic v2 strict mode (extra='forbid', validate_assignment, strict) with field/model validators to enforce dev-lane-only thread namespaces and topology integrity.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Cora handler package init exposing the shared append_checkpoint helper that builds a NodeCheckpoint with SanctumFingerprint and merges it into StoryState.

nodes.name: close_story.py
nodes.summary: Cora dev-graph handler for the close_story node; appends a checkpoint via the shared helper.

nodes.name: implement_story.py
nodes.summary: Cora dev-graph handler for the implement_story node; appends a checkpoint via the shared helper.

nodes.name: plan_story.py
nodes.summary: Cora dev-graph handler for the plan_story node; appends a checkpoint via the shared helper.

nodes.name: review_story.py
nodes.summary: Cora dev-graph handler for the review_story node; appends a checkpoint via the shared helper.

nodes.name: test_story.py
nodes.summary: Cora dev-graph handler for the test_story node; appends a checkpoint via the shared helper.

nodes.name: node_checkpoint.py
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic v2 NodeCheckpoint model capturing per-node story progress with timezone-aware timestamps, status enum, and an embedded sanctum fingerprint.

nodes.name: sanctum_fingerprint.py
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic v2 SanctumFingerprint model recording a UUID4 snapshot id, SHA-256 content digest, and timezone-aware capture time for sanctum state snapshots.

nodes.name: story_state.py
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic v2 StoryState model representing the LangGraph dev-lane story-scoped state with a list of node checkpoints and updated_at timestamp.

nodes.name: node_checkpoint_validators.py
nodes.summary: Cross-field validator enforcing the NodeCheckpoint invariant that a 'complete' status requires a completed_at timestamp and non-complete statuses must leave it unset.

nodes.name: _load_preclosure_hook
nodes.summary: Dynamically loads the preclosure hook module from the bmad-agent-cora skills directory using importlib.util, returning the PreClosureResult class and run_preclosure_check function.

nodes.name: block_mode_node
nodes.summary: LangGraph node that runs the Cora pre-closure check against StoryState and raises a GateError when closure is denied; otherwise updates updated_at.

nodes.name: DevGraphNodeSpec
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic model describing a single Cora dev-graph node with id, label, and dotted handler reference.

nodes.name: DevGraphEdgeSpec
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic model describing one directed edge in the dev-graph manifest, aliasing 'from' to from_node to avoid the Python keyword.

nodes.name: DevGraphManifest
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic manifest for the Cora dev-lane graph that validates schema version, node/edge topology, and enforces that thread_namespace targets the dev/ lane with a {story_id} placeholder.

nodes.name: CompiledGraphHandle
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass bundling a compiled LangGraph graph, its source manifest, and a thread-namespace template with a per-story formatter method.

nodes.name: _resolve_handler
nodes.summary: Resolves a dotted 'module:attr' handler reference into a callable, raising CompileError on bad format, missing module/attribute, or non-callable target.

nodes.name: load_dev_graph_manifest
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the dev-graph YAML manifest from disk, raising ManifestValidationError for missing files, malformed YAML, or schema violations.

nodes.name: _build_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs a LangGraph StateGraph[StoryState] by adding each manifest node with its resolved handler and wiring edges, defaulting to START when no explicit start edge exists.

nodes.name: compile_dev_graph
nodes.summary: Top-level entry that coerces a manifest input (path or model), builds the StateGraph, compiles it, and returns a CompiledGraphHandle.

nodes.name: append_checkpoint
nodes.summary: Builds a complete NodeCheckpoint (with SanctumFingerprint derived from a SHA-256 of node_id) and returns the StoryState patch appending it plus refreshing updated_at.

nodes.name: NodeCheckpoint
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 model representing one node's checkpoint with status enum, mandatory tz-aware timestamps, optional completed_at, and a model-level invariant requiring completed_at iff status is 'complete'.

nodes.name: SanctumFingerprint
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 model carrying a UUID4 snapshot_id, 64-char SHA-256 content_sha256, and tz-aware captured_at, with field validators enforcing UUID version and timezone-awareness.

nodes.name: StoryState
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 model for the LangGraph dev-lane state keyed by story_id, holding the ordered list of NodeCheckpoint entries and a tz-aware updated_at.

nodes.name: enforce_complete_requires_completed_at
nodes.summary: Cross-field validator that raises ValueError when status=='complete' is missing completed_at, or when a non-complete status carries a completed_at value.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel module for the gate-layer package exposing error types, operator verdict schema, party-mode-as-interrupt entry, and resume-API helpers as the canonical public surface.

nodes.name: guardrails.py
nodes.summary: Import-time and AST-source guardrails that prevent scheduler/bypass modules from being loaded or referenced inside gate-layer code, enforcing the no-scheduler invariant.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ast.walk to statically scan for forbidden import patterns; runtime sys.modules check complements the static pass.

nodes.name: verdict.py
nodes.summary: Thin gate-layer re-export of OperatorVerdict/OperatorVerdictVerb that runs scheduler-import guardrails before exposing the schema.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: HTTP bridge package scaffold that re-exports the gate-verdict endpoint as the package's public entry point.

nodes.name: gate_endpoint.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI app factory and endpoint handlers for gate verdict submission, override submission, and override application — the HTTP transport surface for the gate layer.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses FastAPI Header-injected operator_id, HTTPException-based error mapping, and Pydantic model_validate at the edge.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel module for the learning-ledger package surfacing emitter, event-builder, and query helpers as the canonical public API.

nodes.name: emitter.py
nodes.summary: Postgres-backed learning-ledger emitter with idempotent insert semantics, schema-bootstrap helper, failure-counter metrics, and DATABASE_URL resolution.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses psycopg connection + JSONB payload; idempotency key short-circuits duplicate inserts to the same row.

nodes.name: events.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 ledger-event schemas (VerdictLedgerEvent, OverrideLedgerEvent, SanctumMutationLedgerEvent) with UUID4/tz-aware validators, canonical natural-key derivation, and SHA-256 idempotency-key computation.
nodes.languageNotes: Inheritance hierarchy under _LedgerEventBase; idempotency_key derived deterministically from natural_key via hashlib.sha256.

nodes.name: queries.py
nodes.summary: Read-side helpers for the learning ledger: reject-rate-per-gate aggregation, gate inventory listing, and sanctum-mutation event hydration with Pydantic deserialization.
nodes.languageNotes: All queries accept an optional pre-opened psycopg connection to support transactional tests.

nodes.name: gate_decide.py
nodes.summary: MCP-server tool wrappers (gate_decide_tool, override_submit_tool, override_apply_tool) that forward operator verdicts and override flows through the same resume/override APIs the HTTP endpoint uses.

nodes.name: override_api.py
nodes.summary: In-process override registry and orchestration: computes cache-invalidation impact, mints confirm tokens, applies operator verdicts to RunState, and emits OverrideLedgerEvent records to the ledger.
nodes.languageNotes: Module-level registries (_RUN_STATE_REGISTRY, _PENDING_WARNINGS, _CONSUMED_TOKENS) act as test-resettable in-memory state; SHA-256 confirm tokens bind warning to apply call.

nodes.name: override_warning.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 ModelOverrideWarning schema carrying cache-impact estimate, affected-node set, confirm token, and validated expires_at>issued_at invariant.

nodes.name: sanctum_watcher.py
nodes.summary: Filesystem watcher (watchdog + polling fallback) that detects sanctum markdown mutations during an active trial invocation, emits SanctumMutationLedgerEvent records, and queues pending SanctumWarning entries per trial.
nodes.languageNotes: Threading-based polling thread complements watchdog observer; SHA-256 hashes normalize CRLF to LF for cross-platform stability.

nodes.name: assert_scheduler_modules_not_loaded
nodes.summary: Raises SchedulerImportError if any scheduler-prefixed module is already present in sys.modules at import time.

nodes.name: assert_no_scheduler_or_bypass_source
nodes.summary: AST-walks a source string to reject scheduler/bypass imports and unauthorized-bypass-call patterns, raising SchedulerImportError or UnauthorizedResumeBypassError.

nodes.name: gate_verdict_endpoint
nodes.summary: Validates an OperatorVerdict payload, resumes the workflow, and builds the transport response for the verdict endpoint.

nodes.name: gate_override_submit_endpoint
nodes.summary: Forwards an override submission request to submit_override and returns the resulting ModelOverrideWarning as a JSON dump.

nodes.name: gate_override_apply_endpoint
nodes.summary: Applies an override verdict via apply_override and returns the resulting OverrideEvent together with refreshed decision-card metadata.

nodes.name: create_gate_app
nodes.summary: FastAPI app factory that wires the three gate POST routes with operator-id header injection and HTTPException-based error mapping.

nodes.name: EmissionResult
nodes.summary: Frozen result record returned by emit_ledger_event capturing status (inserted/duplicate/failed), event_id, idempotency_key, and failure reason.

nodes.name: emit_ledger_event
nodes.summary: Inserts a ledger event into Postgres with idempotency-key deduplication, schema bootstrap, JSONB payload serialization, and failure-counter increments on errors.

nodes.name: ensure_ledger_schema
nodes.summary: Executes the bundled ledger schema DDL against the provided connection to lazily create the ledger table.

nodes.name: _load_existing_event_id
nodes.summary: Looks up an existing ledger event_id for a given idempotency_key to support deduplicated inserts.

nodes.name: _LedgerEventBase
nodes.summary: Abstract Pydantic base for ledger events providing UUID4/tz-aware validators, payload-extraction, and SHA-256 idempotency_key derivation from a subclass-defined natural_key.

nodes.name: VerdictLedgerEvent
nodes.summary: Ledger event recording an operator gate verdict with verb, card_id, verdict_id, operator_id, and transport_kind metadata.

nodes.name: OverrideLedgerEvent
nodes.summary: Ledger event recording an operator model-override action across the submit/apply phases with confirm_token linkage.

nodes.name: SanctumMutationLedgerEvent
nodes.summary: Ledger event recording a detected mutation of a sanctum markdown file with before/after SHA-256 hashes and suggested invalidating commit.

nodes.name: build_verdict_ledger_event
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs a VerdictLedgerEvent from an OperatorVerdict instance and the originating transport kind.

nodes.name: build_override_ledger_event
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs an OverrideLedgerEvent for either the submit or apply phase of a model-override action.

nodes.name: build_sanctum_mutation_ledger_event
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs a SanctumMutationLedgerEvent for a detected file mutation.

nodes.name: reject_rate_per_gate
nodes.summary: Aggregates verdict events by gate_id for a trial, returning reject-vs-total counts per gate.

nodes.name: gate_inventory
nodes.summary: Returns the distinct gate_id inventory along with first/last-seen timestamps for a trial.

nodes.name: sanctum_mutations
nodes.summary: Retrieves sanctum-mutation ledger events for a trial, hydrating them back into SanctumMutationLedgerEvent instances.

nodes.name: gate_decide_tool
nodes.summary: MCP tool entry that validates a verdict payload and resumes the workflow, mirroring the HTTP gate-verdict endpoint.

nodes.name: override_submit_tool
nodes.summary: MCP tool entry that forwards an override submission request and returns the resulting warning record.

nodes.name: override_apply_tool
nodes.summary: MCP tool entry that applies an override verdict and returns the resulting OverrideEvent together with refreshed decision-card metadata.

nodes.name: compute_cache_impact
nodes.summary: Computes the estimated cost delta and affected-node set when proposing a new model for a node, using the cached node graph and model-cost table.

nodes.name: _confirm_token
nodes.summary: Derives a deterministic SHA-256 confirm token from trial_id, node_id, requested model, and current cache state so apply requests can only replay the warning they were issued for.

nodes.name: submit_override
nodes.summary: Records a pending ModelOverrideWarning for a trial/node/model triplet, computes cache impact, mints a confirm token, and emits a phase=submit OverrideLedgerEvent.

nodes.name: apply_override
nodes.summary: Validates a confirm token against the pending warning, mutates RunState to record the override and cache-state transition, and emits a phase=apply OverrideLedgerEvent.

nodes.name: decision_card_meta_for_trial
nodes.summary: Builds the DecisionCardMeta snapshot for a trial including current model overrides, override trail, sanctum warnings, and cache-state label.

nodes.name: ModelOverrideWarning
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 model carrying a model-override warning payload (estimated cost delta, affected nodes, cache-state delta, SHA-256 confirm token, issued/expires timestamps) with validators enforcing UUID4, tz-aware datetimes, and expiry ordering.

nodes.name: _SanctumEventHandler
nodes.summary: watchdog FileSystemEventHandler subclass that delegates on_modified/on_created events back to the owning SanctumWatcher.

nodes.name: SanctumWatcher
nodes.summary: Threading-aware sanctum filesystem watcher combining a watchdog observer with a polling-thread fallback; tracks per-file SHA-256 hashes across invocations and emits SanctumMutationLedgerEvents plus per-trial SanctumWarning queue entries when content changes.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the Section 02a composer; re-exports Directive models, ComposerCache, and the compose entry point as the public API.

nodes.name: _cache.py
nodes.summary: Defines a small in-memory prompt-response cache used to deduplicate LLM classification calls during directive composition.

nodes.name: ComposerCache
nodes.summary: Lightweight dict-backed cache mapping prompt hashes to LLM responses, exposing get/set methods for the composer.

nodes.name: _prompt.py
nodes.summary: Jinja2-backed prompt rendering for corpus-file classification, with deterministic prompt normalization and SHA-256 cache-key derivation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Jinja2 FileSystemLoader to render classification prompts; cache keys are SHA-256 of whitespace-normalized prompt text.

nodes.name: render_classification_prompt
nodes.summary: Renders the per-file classification prompt template with relative path, file size, suffix, and binary hex sample context.

nodes.name: cli_adapter.py
nodes.summary: CLI integration shim that resolves the LLM model, drives directive composition via compose(), and persists the directive YAML plus model-resolution trail to a run directory.

nodes.name: compose_and_write
nodes.summary: End-to-end CLI helper that classifies the corpus, writes the directive YAML, captures its SHA-256 digest, and records the model-resolution audit trail for the run.

nodes.name: composer.py
nodes.summary: Core Section 02a composer: walks the corpus directory, applies rule-based exclusions, classifies remaining files via an LLM, and assembles a Directive Pydantic model serialized to YAML.
nodes.languageNotes: Couples deterministic corpus walking and rule-based exclusion with LLM-driven classification; emits Pydantic-validated Directive YAML.

nodes.name: DirectiveCompositionError
nodes.summary: Domain exception raised when directive composition fails (missing corpus directory, malformed LLM payload, etc.).

nodes.name: compose
nodes.summary: Top-level composer entry point: iterates corpus files, applies exclusions, classifies via LLM with caching, and returns a fully validated Directive model.

nodes.name: _classify_with_llm
nodes.summary: Classifies a single corpus file by rendering the prompt, consulting the cache, invoking the LLM on miss, and parsing the JSON payload into a DirectiveSource.

nodes.name: write_directive_yaml
nodes.summary: Serializes a Directive model to YAML on disk, ensuring the parent directory exists before writing.

nodes.name: _walk_corpus_files
nodes.summary: Validates the corpus directory exists and recursively yields sorted file paths for downstream classification.

nodes.name: _ignored_source
nodes.summary: Builds a DirectiveSource entry tagged with the role 'ignored' along with the rule-based exclusion reason, bypassing the LLM.

nodes.name: directive_model.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 schema for Section 02a directives: defines DirectiveRole/ExcludedReason enums, the DirectiveSource row model with role-field invariants, and the top-level Directive container with UUID4/tz-aware validation.
nodes.languageNotes: Applies Pydantic v2 triple-layer red-rejection on closed enums and reuses enforce_uuid4_version / enforce_tz_aware shared validators.

nodes.name: DirectiveRole
nodes.summary: Closed enum of directive roles (e.g., source, ignored) used to classify each corpus file in the directive.

nodes.name: ExcludedReason
nodes.summary: Closed enum of allowed exclusion reasons for ignored corpus files (e.g., known-binary, hidden-dotfile).

nodes.name: DirectiveSource
nodes.summary: Pydantic row model for a single corpus file in a directive, enforcing role-specific field invariants and rejecting unknown role/excluded_reason values.

nodes.name: Directive
nodes.summary: Top-level directive container with UUID4 run_id, tz-aware composed_at timestamp, corpus_dir, list of DirectiveSources, and schema version.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the Section 02a operator gate; re-exports the poll-surface entry points (submit_verdict, resume_from_verdict, display_directive, etc.).

nodes.name: _transports.py
nodes.summary: Per-transport adapters (CLI, HTTP, MCP-stdio) that wrap submit_verdict and produce a uniform transport response with directive display, verdict payload, and resumed state.

nodes.name: submit_cli_verdict
nodes.summary: CLI-surface adapter that constructs a Section02AOperatorVerdict and returns the standard transport response envelope.

nodes.name: submit_http_verdict
nodes.summary: HTTP-surface adapter that constructs a Section02AOperatorVerdict and returns the standard transport response envelope.

nodes.name: submit_mcp_stdio_verdict
nodes.summary: MCP-stdio surface adapter that constructs a Section02AOperatorVerdict and returns the standard transport response envelope.

nodes.name: poll_surface.py
nodes.summary: Canonical operator-gate poll surface for Section 02a: loads directives, computes canonical model digests, applies edits, validates verdicts, and resumes the run with the operator decision.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses deterministic JSON canonicalization plus SHA-256 to bind verdicts to a specific directive snapshot for tamper detection.

nodes.name: submit_verdict
nodes.summary: Validates an operator verb (accept/edit/reject), applies any edit payload, and returns a Section02AOperatorVerdict bound to the canonical directive digest.

nodes.name: resume_from_verdict
nodes.summary: Re-verifies the verdict digest against the current directive and returns the resumed directive plus verdict payload for downstream pipeline continuation.

nodes.name: apply_directive_edit
nodes.summary: Applies a DirectiveEditPayload to a Directive, rejecting unknown ref_ids and re-validating the edited Directive through Pydantic.

nodes.name: canonical_model_bytes
nodes.summary: Serializes a Pydantic model to deterministic canonical JSON bytes (sorted keys, compact separators, ASCII-safe) for digest stability.

nodes.name: compute_model_digest
nodes.summary: Computes SHA-256 hex digest of a model's canonical JSON form, used to bind operator verdicts to a specific directive snapshot.

nodes.name: load_directive
nodes.summary: Reads a directive YAML file from disk and parses it through Directive.model_validate for full Pydantic re-validation.

nodes.name: display_directive
nodes.summary: Returns a decision-card view of the directive including its canonical digest for operator review.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_02a.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 models for the Section 02a operator verdict surface: DirectiveEditPayload (per-source field updates) and Section02AOperatorVerdict (verb + digest + payload with verb/payload consistency invariants).
nodes.languageNotes: Enforces SHA-256 hex shape on decision_card_digest plus mutually-exclusive verb/payload invariants (edit requires edit_payload; reject requires reject_reason).

nodes.name: DirectiveEditPayload
nodes.summary: Pydantic model carrying a mapping of ref_id to per-field updates for directive sources, rejecting empty update blocks.

nodes.name: Section02AOperatorVerdict
nodes.summary: Pydantic verdict record (run_id, surface_id, verb, operator_id, submitted_at, decision_card_digest, optional edit_payload/reject_reason) enforcing UUID4, tz-aware, SHA-256, and verb/payload consistency invariants.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the audit-chain integrity helpers; re-exports the verifier function and the three audit-chain exception classes (FR-7c-50).
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel — uses explicit __all__ to define the public audit-chain API.

nodes.name: chain.py
nodes.summary: Pure audit-chain integrity check that validates monotonic per-tripwire fired_at ordering and required trace_id linkage for fired/marginal-fired verdicts in the TripwireLedgerEntry stream.
nodes.languageNotes: Stateless verification function with no I/O; raises typed AuditChain* exceptions for downstream callers to surface as governance failures.

nodes.name: errors.py
nodes.summary: Defines the audit-chain integrity exception hierarchy (root + ordering + parent-link variants) referenced by FR-7c-50 chain verification logic.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Shared model package barrel for Slab 1; re-exports the TripwireId enum, TripwireSeverity enum, and TripwireLedgerEntry Pydantic model.

nodes.name: tripwire_ledger.py
nodes.summary: Canonical Pydantic-v2 schema for Slab 7c tripwire-ledger rows (NFR-7c-OD2), including the closed TripwireId enum (TW-7c-1..6), the severity tier enum, and the TripwireLedgerEntry model with triple-layer red-rejection on tripwire_id, tz-aware fired_at, and UUID4 trace_id enforcement.
nodes.languageNotes: Applies the project Pydantic-v2 schema checklist: validate_assignment=True, extra='forbid', TypeAdapter-based explicit coercion-path red-rejection on the closed Literal alias, and field validators that delegate tz/UUID4 enforcement to app.models.state._base helpers.

nodes.name: verify_audit_chain
nodes.summary: Iterates over an ordered list of TripwireLedgerEntry rows and raises AuditChainOrderError on non-monotonic fired_at per tripwire_id or AuditChainParentLinkError when a fired/marginal-fired verdict lacks a trace_id.

nodes.name: AuditChainIntegrityError
nodes.summary: Root exception type for audit-chain integrity violations (FR-7c-50); parent of the ordering and parent-link error subclasses.

nodes.name: AuditChainOrderError
nodes.summary: Raised when a TripwireLedgerEntry breaks monotonic fired_at ordering for its tripwire_id in the ledger.

nodes.name: AuditChainParentLinkError
nodes.summary: Raised when a fired or marginal-fired verdict row is missing the parent trace_id required for cross-trial provenance.

nodes.name: TripwireId
nodes.summary: Closed StrEnum of the six Slab 7c tripwire identifiers (TW-7c-1 through TW-7c-6) used as a typed key in the ledger schema.

nodes.name: TripwireSeverity
nodes.summary: Closed StrEnum of severity tiers (critical / high / medium) attached to each TripwireLedgerEntry.

nodes.name: TripwireLedgerEntry
nodes.summary: Canonical Pydantic-v2 BaseModel for tripwire-ledger rows: enforces closed tripwire_id via TypeAdapter coercion-path red-rejection, tz-aware fired_at, optional UUID4 trace_id, plus structured severity, verdict, and decision-record fields per Murat A5 / NFR-7c-OD2.
nodes.languageNotes: ConfigDict pins validate_assignment=True and extra='forbid'; pre-mode validator on tripwire_id implements the explicit triple-layer red-rejection idiom from the Pydantic-v2 schema checklist.

nodes.name: _shared.py
nodes.summary: Shared envelope dataclasses (Invocation, ReturnEnvelope, LandingPoint, CapabilityError) and dual-mode (summarize|execute) CLI harness used by every Marcus PR-* production-readiness capability script.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses frozen Python dataclasses with Literal types to pin the invocation/return envelope contracts (AC-C.1/C.2 from Story 26-6).

nodes.name: InvocationContext
nodes.summary: Optional context payload (run_id, bundle_path, invoked_by) that Marcus passes into a capability invocation.

nodes.name: Invocation
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass representing the canonical 6-field invocation envelope (capability_code, mode, args, context, idempotency_key) Marcus sends to a capability.

nodes.name: LandingPoint
nodes.summary: Dataclass describing where a capability's output landed on disk: bundle_path, manifest, and sha256 hash for verification.

nodes.name: CapabilityError
nodes.summary: Structured error record (code, message, remediation) appended to a ReturnEnvelope when a capability fails at the capability level.

nodes.name: ReturnEnvelope
nodes.summary: The canonical 7-field return envelope (status, capability_code, run_id, result, landing_point, errors, telemetry) emitted by capabilities; to_dict serializes optional fields cleanly.

nodes.name: stub_envelope
nodes.summary: Constructs a canonical NOT_YET_IMPLEMENTED ReturnEnvelope used by capability stubs (PR-HC, PR-RS) prior to full implementation.

nodes.name: run_cli
nodes.summary: Dual-mode CLI harness: parses args, dispatches to summarize_fn or execute_fn, attaches telemetry, and prints the ReturnEnvelope as JSON to stdout.

nodes.name: pr_hc.py
nodes.summary: PR-HC (Health Check) capability stub — registered with pinned contracts and a NOT_YET_IMPLEMENTED envelope; full implementation deferred to story 26-10.

nodes.name: summarize
nodes.summary: Summarize-mode entry point returning the canonical PR-HC NOT_YET_IMPLEMENTED stub envelope.

nodes.name: execute
nodes.summary: Execute-mode entry point returning the canonical PR-HC NOT_YET_IMPLEMENTED stub envelope (identical to summarize until story 26-10).

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that dispatches PR-HC summarize/execute through the shared run_cli harness.

nodes.name: pr_pf.py
nodes.summary: PR-PF (Preflight) capability — wraps scripts.utilities.app_session_readiness as a subprocess to give Marcus a structured readiness report before firing Prompt 1 on a tracked production run.
nodes.languageNotes: Verbose-posture: summarize-mode reports what WOULD happen; execute-mode invokes the readiness subprocess with a 120-second timeout.

nodes.name: summarize
nodes.summary: Reports the preflight plan (target receipt path, subprocess command, flags) without touching disk or running subprocesses.

nodes.name: _build_cmd
nodes.summary: Constructs the argv list invoking scripts.utilities.app_session_readiness with the preflight flags resolved from args/bundle_path.

nodes.name: _resolve_session_receipt_path
nodes.summary: Resolves the on-disk path for the session preflight receipt — either an explicit override from args or the default name under bundle_path.

nodes.name: _run_subprocess
nodes.summary: Invokes the readiness subprocess with a bounded timeout and captures stdout/stderr/returncode for downstream envelope assembly.

nodes.name: execute
nodes.summary: Runs the readiness subprocess, classifies failure modes (timeout, non-JSON output, non-zero exit, receipt-write failure) into structured CapabilityErrors, and writes the session receipt to disk.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that dispatches PR-PF summarize/execute through the shared run_cli harness.

nodes.name: pr_rc.py
nodes.summary: PR-RC (Run-Constants author + validate) capability — Marcus authors canonical lowercase-nested run-constants.yaml directly, eliminating the hand-transcription schema-drift vector that caused the 2026-04-17 Tejal trial halt at Prompt 1.
nodes.languageNotes: Two sub-modes under execute: 'author' writes the YAML; 'validate' parses an existing file through scripts.utilities.run_constants.parse_run_constants.

nodes.name: _normalize_values
nodes.summary: Normalizes operator-supplied values dict by mapping legacy UPPERCASE keys to canonical snake_case and recursively lower-casing nested keys.

nodes.name: _canonical_ordered
nodes.summary: Produces a deterministically ordered dict for YAML emission: required fields first in canonical order, then any remaining keys sorted alphabetically.

nodes.name: summarize
nodes.summary: Dry-run reporter: validates that supplied values would parse as RunConstants and renders the canonical YAML preview without touching disk.

nodes.name: _target_path
nodes.summary: Resolves the target YAML path from args.target_path or InvocationContext.bundle_path, raising ValueError when neither is supplied.

nodes.name: _author
nodes.summary: Author sub-mode: validates input, renders canonical YAML, computes sha256, writes the file to disk, and returns a ReturnEnvelope with a LandingPoint manifest.

nodes.name: _validate
nodes.summary: Validate sub-mode: loads an existing run-constants.yaml from disk and runs it through parse_run_constants, classifying failures into structured CapabilityErrors.

nodes.name: execute
nodes.summary: Execute-mode dispatcher: routes to _author or _validate based on args.mode_sub after normalizing input values.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that dispatches PR-RC summarize/execute through the shared run_cli harness.

nodes.name: pr_rs.py

nodes.name: ready_for_trial.sh

nodes.name: Invoke-Step

nodes.summary: Invokes the venv python with an inline heredoc that loads .env via scripts.utilities.env_loader and asserts that OPENAI_API_KEY and LANGSMITH_API_KEY are set in the environment.

nodes.name: run_step
nodes.summary: Shell counterpart of Invoke-Step: runs the given command with arguments, records the exit code into STEP_RESULTS, remembers the first failed step in FAILED_STEP, and prints PASS or FAIL.

nodes.name: _normalize_scalar
nodes.summary: Lowercases and regex-scrubs run IDs, UUIDs, and timestamps from a scalar so that replay differences in identity-only fields do not pollute structural match scores.

nodes.summary: Recursively flattens a nested mapping into sorted dotted-key lines, indexing list elements positionally, to produce a comparable line set.

nodes.name: _compare_semantic_lines
nodes.summary: Diffs two flattened line sets, computes a structural match score over the larger set, and emits a divergence rationale listing missing keys per side.

nodes.name: _canonical_motion_plan
nodes.summary: Extracts the canonical motion-plan fields (lesson slug, motion-budget bounds, present flag) from a replay-side payload for parity comparison.

nodes.name: compare_actual_substrate_parity
nodes.summary: Top-level comparator that walks every ArtifactParitySpec, applies per-side canonicalizers, scores presence and structural match, and assembles the ParityComparisonReport.

nodes.name: render_parity_evidence_markdown
nodes.summary: Renders a parity report as operator-facing markdown evidence with header, per-artifact tables, and divergence rationale sections.

nodes.name: write_parity_evidence_markdown
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper that calls the renderer and writes the resulting markdown bytes to the supplied output path.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses primary/clone/baseline paths and an optional output path, runs the comparison, and writes the evidence markdown.

tour.title: Project Orientation: Skills Index and Environment
tour.description: Start at the project's outward-facing surface. The skills/ index advertises the BMAD agent ecosystem that wraps the runtime, while .env.example enumerates every secret the hybrid stack expects — OpenAI, LangSmith, Postgres, ElevenLabs, Canva, Notion, and more. Reading these together answers the first newcomer question: 'what does this project do, and what does it need to run?' The README excerpt frames the project as collaborative-intelligence infrastructure for course-content production; these two files make that statement concrete.

tour.title: Operator Entry Point: The Marcus CLI
tour.description: Every production trial begins at `python -m app.marcus.cli`. The `__main__.py` dispatcher wires the three top-level subcommands — `trial`, `adhoc`, and gate operations — into a single argparse parser. `trial.py` implements the lifecycle (G0 directive confirm → run_production_trial → resume on verdict), and `gate_cli.py` is the bridge operators use to submit gate verdicts and override payloads from JSON. This is the surface the README's one-line elevator pitch terminates at.
tour.languageLesson: Python's `__main__.py` makes a package directly runnable with `python -m <package>`. It's the idiomatic way to package a CLI without shipping a separate console-script entry — the package itself is the executable.

tour.title: Marcus Orchestrator: Driving Trials Through Gates
tour.description: Once the CLI hands off, control lands in the production orchestrator. `production_runner.py` (the highest fan-out node in the codebase at 71 outgoing edges) is the engine: it compiles the manifest, dispatches specialists between gates, builds decision cards, persists conversation turns, and supports resume-from-verdict. `m3_trial.py` is the local trial runner that exercises G1-G4 with stub cards, while `pre_packet.py` shows how Marcus prepares the Irene handoff envelope with SHA-256 digests of source materials.

tour.title: The Pipeline Manifest: Declarative Graph Assembly
tour.description: Marcus doesn't hardcode the production flow — it compiles it from a YAML pipeline manifest. `schema.py` defines the Pydantic v2 `PipelineManifest`, `NodeSpec`, and `EdgeSpec` shapes with cross-field validators; `loader.py` parses the YAML; `compiler.py` resolves dotted handler references and assembles the LangGraph `StateGraph`. This declarative layer is what makes the multi-agent flow reconfigurable: change the manifest, get a different graph. CLAUDE.md's 'pipeline lockstep regime' guards exactly these files.
tour.languageLesson: Pydantic v2 with `model_config = ConfigDict(validate_assignment=True)` rejects invalid mutations after construction, not just at creation. Combined with `Field` constraints and `model_validator`, it gives YAML configs the same red-rejection discipline as code-level types.

tour.title: The Specialist Scaffold: 9-Node RPAVRSGFH Pattern
tour.description: Every specialist (14 of them) is built from the same 9-node LangGraph template: receive → plan → act → verify → reflect → emit_spans → gate → finalize → handoff. `_scaffold/contract.py` defines the conformance contract and node-set; `_scaffold/graph.py` is the reference builder that asserts reachability and installs the interrupt at the gate node. This uniformity is what lets Marcus dispatch any specialist by id without special-casing — the gate interrupt is where HIL DecisionCards surface.

tour.title: Three Specialists in Practice: Irene, Quinn-R, Compositor
tour.description: The same 9-node scaffold instantiates into very different agents. Irene runs a two-pass acting loop for lesson-prose authoring (highest fan-out specialist). Quinn-R is the quality reviewer that gates revision verdicts — approve / revise / decline. The Compositor is deterministic: its `_act.py` copies visual and motion assets into bundles and emits a content-addressed hash. Together they show the breadth — LLM authoring, evaluative gating, deterministic assembly — that the uniform scaffold absorbs.

tour.title: Gates, HIL, and Party-Mode-as-Interrupt
tour.description: The gate layer is the human-in-the-loop frontier. `resume_api.py` registers decision cards with cryptographic digests, validates server-issued nonces, and emits ledger events when a verdict resumes the LangGraph run. `hil_intake.py` converts operator-pre-collected intake into the orchestrator's callable contract. `party_mode_as_interrupt.py` is the most striking design choice: multi-agent BMAD party-mode deliberation is itself modeled as a gate interrupt, so consensus rounds resume the graph exactly like any other operator verdict. `errors.py` enumerates the bypass-attempt exceptions that protect this surface.

tour.title: Decision Cards: Tamper-Evident Operator Verdicts
tour.description: Every gate hands the operator a typed DecisionCard. `_base.py` defines the shared `DecisionCardMeta` (cache-state + override-trail + affected-nodes) and the digest-bearing `DecisionCardBase`. G1 covers estimator/plan-unit opening, G2C carries readiness status and blocking issues, G3 reports progress with operator prompt, and G4 records final outcome with artifact paths. The matching JSON Schemas in `schema/` are emitted from these Pydantic models so external clients (Canvas, MCP) can validate verdicts without importing Python. This is the README's 'tamper-evident operator-verdict' substrate.
tour.languageLesson: JSON Schema files emitted from Pydantic v2 (`model_json_schema()`) serve as the cross-language contract. The Python source-of-truth and the JSON artifact must stay in lockstep — a common migration anti-pattern is editing only one side. Triple-layer red-rejection (Field constraint + validator + closed enum) is the project's defense against schema drift.

tour.title: RunState and Envelopes: The Pydantic Source-of-Truth
tour.description: The 160-node domain-models layer is the project's bedrock. `_base.py` enforces timezone-aware datetimes and UUID4 version across every state model (fan-in 47 — the most depended-upon file in the codebase). `run_state.py` is the authoritative LangGraph `RunState` root: UUID4 run id, lifecycle timestamps, model-resolution trail, sanctum/marcus fingerprints, story states. `SpecialistEnvelope` carries every cross-boundary payload, and `OperatorVerdict` enforces no-tamper verbs and verb-payload consistency. If you change a shape here, you change the entire system.

tour.title: Parity Contracts and the Cora Dev-Graph
tour.description: Two adjacent substrate systems keep the runtime honest. The parity-contracts package decorates surfaces with `@parity_contract`, registering them in a typed registry so audit, sanctum, and reference-surface checks can enforce drift-free transport semantics across the HTTP, MCP, and in-process surfaces. Cora is the BMAD dev-session orchestrator implemented as its own LangGraph: `cora/graph.py` compiles a story-state machine (plan → implement → test → review → close) from a YAML dev-graph manifest, and `block_mode_node.py` is the pre-closure guard that refuses to close a story until the BMAD preclosure hook clears.

tour.title: Runtime Infrastructure: Checkpointer, Ledger, FastAPI, MCP
tour.description: Underneath the orchestrator sits the durability and transport layer. `checkpointer.py` wires LangGraph's AsyncPostgresSaver to `DATABASE_URL`. The learning ledger emits `VerdictLedgerEvent`, `OverrideLedgerEvent`, and `SanctumMutationLedgerEvent` records with idempotent inserts and SHA-256 natural-key derivation — this is the README's 'learning ledger.' Two parallel transports surface the gate API: FastAPI (`http/gate_endpoint.py` plus `runtime/server.py` with /health and /invoke), and MCP stdio (`mcp_server/server.py` plus the `gate_decide` tool wrappers). Both routes converge on the same resume/override APIs, which is what the parity contracts from Step 10 enforce.

tour.title: BMAD Skills and the v4.2 Pack Generator
tour.description: The capstone is the ecosystem around the runtime. Each specialist has a corresponding BMAD persona SKILL.md (88 nodes across the agent-skills layer) — Cora orchestrates dev sessions, Gary delegates to Gamma for slide generation. These skills are activated via the `_bmad/memory/bmad-agent-<name>/` sanctum trees referenced in CLAUDE.md. Parallel to the runtime, the v4.2 prompt-pack generator (`scripts/generators/v42/`) renders a complete operator-facing lesson-planning pack from the same pipeline manifest, using Jinja2 templates per section. Together, the runtime executes the work, the skills define the personas that author/review it, and the v4.2 pack is the printable contract operators read during a live trial.
tour.languageLesson: Jinja2 with `StrictUndefined` raises on any unresolved variable instead of silently rendering empty — combined with a deterministic environment factory and fixed newline handling, this produces byte-stable pack outputs that hash identically across runs. Reproducibility at the template layer is what lets the 'frozen-graph reproducibility ceremony' work end-to-end.
